question: Final recheck R1. Read-only. Inspect final revised docs: <machine-local-path> docs\OS_COMPLETION_CONDITIONS.md, docs\VERIFICATION.md, AGENTS.md, and memory summary if needed. Lens: user intent and completion bar. Question: after latest R3/R5 fixes and consensus record update, does the plan still match the user's target closed-loop DinoBrain OS without falsely claiming implementation complete? Return: reviewer_id=R1; verdict=AGREE or REVISE_REQUIRED; blocking_objections=[...]; required_doc_changes=[...]; evidence_files=[...].

items.title: Current user instruction outranks stored memory
items.summary: For DinoBrain and Codex work, current user instructions must take priority over OS memory, Context Packs, and previous decisions. | Rule: Use memory as subordinate evidence; if the current user instruction conflicts with stored memory, follow the current instruction and record the correction if durable. | Review: review_status=accepted_by_agent_review

items.title: Prefer direct local verification and evidence
items.summary: The user prefers direct inspection of local files, logs, command output, screenshots, repository state, and concrete evidence over abstract claims. | Rule: For verification/debugging, inspect the real workspace and report command/file evidence; if a check was not run, say that plainly. | Review: review_status=accepted_by_agent_review

items.title: Design tasks need high-quality visual iteration without unrelated logic changes
items.summary: For design tasks, the user wants careful high-quality visual work, screenshot-based iteration, and no unrelated logic-code changes when the task is design-only. | Rule: For design-only requests, first restate the visual intent, keep logic untouched, iterate with screenshots, and remove forced motifs when they reduce clarity. | Review: review_status=accepted_by_agent_review

items.title: Plan-first execution for new or handoff-heavy work
items.summary: The user prefers a concrete plan and approved scope before broad implementation, especially for handoff bundles, new projects, difficult design tasks, or OS-level changes. | Rule: When a task is broad, new, design-heavy, or handoff-driven, first summarize understanding, ask only necessary questions, lock the plan, then execute within that plan. | Review: review_status=accepted_by_agent_review

items.title: Carry authorized work through commit, push, and deploy
items.summary: When the user explicitly authorizes implementation or deployment, they expect Codex to carry the work through commit, push, release, and verification rather than stopping at a proposal. | Rule: After explicit authorization such as go, push, deploy, or auto-commit, implement, verify, commit, push, and report exact hashes or links. | Review: review_status=accepted_by_agent_review

items.title: DinoBrain goal is knowledge compounding from sessions
items.summary: The core DinoBrain goal is a compounding LLM Wiki where user sessions become reviewed preferences, decisions, rules, corrections, and source-backed knowledge that improve future sessions. | Rule: Treat sessions as the root source; convert them into reviewed memory, lifecycle-clean noisy memory, evaluate behavior lift, and retrieve the result in later and future Context Packs. | Review: review_status=accepted_by_agent_review

items.title: Installer must restore equivalent DinoBrain behavior on a new PC
items.summary: The user expects a new PC install to clone/update app and data repos, configure Codex and Claude hooks/MCP, build indexes, launch Observatory, and preserve version parity. | Rule: For installer work, verify app/data refs, portable Node, build/index, Codex and Claude registration, hook approval flow, launchers, release artifact alignment, and reinstall/idempotent behavior. | Review: review_status=accepted_by_agent_review
